query:
=== Recent conversation (last 0 exchanges) ===
[Assistant] That's a deeper concern — a feedback loop in the training dynamics itself. If certain categories dominate sessions historically, the GNN learns to score them higher, those entries get injected more, which generates more training signal for those categories, which reinforces the bias. Entries from underrepresented categories never get injected, never get feedback, and the model never learns their value.

This could be connected to `query_count` too — if it was intended as a normalizer for `category_counts` (rate per search rather than raw count), omitting it or getting it wrong could make the saturation worse.

Sending the researcher to check whether ASS-031's GNN architecture addresses this.
=== End recent conversation ===

entries.title: query_log as GNN training reconstruction path — no snapshot table needed for query_count
Exposure bias in confidence-weighted retrieval: never-surfaced entries cannot bootstrap feedback signals
query_log tool coverage: search-only, no context_get or context_lookup rows
NLI neutral-zone score is the detection signal for cross-category institutional memory edges
ADR-002 nan-008: ICD Uses Raw Shannon Entropy with Max-Value Label in Report
